primary_source.system: City Clerk Electronic Legislative Management System (eLMS) — custom Microsoft Power Platform / Dynamics 365 backend, publicly documented with Swagger 2.0
primary_source.how_to_enumerate_meetings: GET https://api.chicityclerkelms.chicago.gov/meeting-agenda?filter=body%20eq%20%27City%20Council%27&sort=date%20desc&top=100&skip=0  — returns paginated JSON with meetingId, date, status, agenda items, and file attachments. Use `top` + `skip` to page through all 295 City Council meetings on record.

second_source.system: Journal of the Proceedings — official statutory record published by the Chicago City Clerk after every City Council meeting, hosted as PDFs on AWS S3 (chicityclerk.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com). Index page: https://www.chicityclerk.com/legislation-records/journals-and-reports/journals-proceedings
second_source.what_it_affirms: Independently produced statutory record containing: synopsis of introductions, full text of adopted legislation, roll call votes, attendance, motions, and parliamentary actions. Verified: the March 18 2026 PDF opened and shows 'JOURNAL of the PROCEEDINGS of the CITY COUNCIL of the CITY of CHICAGO, ILLINOIS — Regular Meeting -- Wednesday, March 18, 2026 — OFFICIAL RECORD.' Table of contents lists committee reports, Agreed Calendar, and roll call vote sections. Produced independently of the eLMS API and can cross-validate item-level votes.

votes_located: Per-item roll-call votes are available via the eLMS REST API at GET /meeting-agenda/{meetingId}/matter/{lineId}/votes. Each record returns voterName, vote value, and personId. Verified example from meeting M26-0001511 (City Council, July 15 2026), matter O2026-0025382 (Zoning Reclassification, hasVotes=true, voteType='Roll Call'): {\"voterName\": \"Lee, Nicole T.\", \"vote\": \"Yea\", \"personId\": \"E29E5122-7BB7-EC11-983E-001DD8049E83\"} — followed by all 50 alderpersons. Votes are also embedded in /matter records under actions[]/votes[] (filterable on actions/votes/voterName and actions/votes/vote). Roll call votes also appear in the Journal of Proceedings PDF as a second source.

systems_surveyed.status: 200 OK — fully live, returns rich JSON; Swagger 2.0 spec at /swagger.json; no auth required
systems_surveyed.era_covered: December 1, 2010 to present (295 City Council meetings, 3605+ total meetings across all bodies)

systems_surveyed.status: 200 OK — HTML SPA that calls the API; confirms API endpoints and CSV export
Accessible as a web UI but returns 'Invalid parameters!' on most direct page fetches; Legistar Webapi tenant 'chicago' returns HTTP 500 (API likely decommissioned). Replaced by eLMS circa June 2023.
systems_surveyed.era_covered: December 1, 2010 – ~June 2023 (historical archive; new records no longer posted here)

systems_surveyed.status: HTTP 500 — tenant not found / API decommissioned for Chicago
200 OK — index page live; PDFs on S3 verified accessible (2026 through at least 2010)
200 OK — links to Journal of Proceedings and commission minutes; not a separate data system
200 OK — Monthly Committee Reports/Minutes and Attendance/Divided Roll Call Reports as PDFs

access_constraints: No authentication required for the eLMS public API (https://api.chicityclerkelms.chicago.gov). Terms of Service at https://chicityclerkelms.chicago.gov/TermsAndConditions must be reviewed before ingestion. The API is labeled 'BETA' in the Swagger spec. Journal PDFs are open on public AWS S3. Agenda/notice PDFs are hosted on Azure Government Cloud (occprodstoragev1.blob.core.usgovcloudapi.net) and appear to be publicly accessible. No rate-limit headers observed, but should be respected.
open_questions: The eLMS API is labeled 'BETA' — confirm with ClerkELMSSupport@cityofchicago.org whether it is production-stable and if breaking changes are versioned.

The lineId parameter in /meeting-agenda/{meetingId}/matter/{lineId}/votes appears to accept matterId (UUID). Confirm this mapping is stable — the Swagger labels it 'lineId' but the agenda item fields return 'matterId'.

chicago.legistar.com web UI still loads — determine exactly what date range it covers and whether its data was fully migrated into eLMS (the 'legacy identifiers vs new identifiers' issue noted by Chicago Councilmatic suggests a possible gap or dual-ID problem around June 2023).

The eLMS meeting-agenda list endpoint ignores the $top query parameter (returns all facets regardless) — confirm correct pagination parameter name ('top' per Swagger) and whether server-side paging is enforced.
Azure Government blob storage base URL (occprodstoragev1.blob.core.usgovcloudapi.net) for attachment PDFs — confirm public read access does not require SAS tokens or expire.

Monthly Committee Reports/Minutes PDFs on chicityclerk.com — determine if these contain structured vote data or only narrative minutes, and whether they supplement the API vote records for committee (non-full-Council) meetings.
